T1.name: Projection Algebra
T1.statement: P_Π is idempotent (P_Π² = P_Π), self-adjoint, and has rank equal to the number of blocks.

T2.name: Defect Operator Characterization
T2.statement: D_Π = (I - P_Π) K P_Π. D_Π = 0 iff K(V_Π) ⊆ V_Π.

T3.name: Quotient Existence
T3.statement: If D_Π = 0, then there exists a unique deterministic quotient map T̄: X/Π → X/Π such that π ∘ T = T̄ ∘ π.

T4.name: Commutator Fallacy
T4.statement: D_Π = 0 does not imply [P_Π, K] = 0. Minimal counterexamples exist.

T5.name: Kaprekar 55-State Quotient
T5.statement: The 4-digit Kaprekar map with gap observable has a 55-state exact quotient with characteristic polynomial λ⁵³(λ−1)² and Jordan blocks 28·J₁(0) ⊕ 2·J₂(0) ⊕ J₃(0) ⊕ 3·J₆(0) ⊕ 2·J₁(1).

assumptions.A3: Π is a partition of X
assumptions.A4: The projection P_Π is the orthogonal projection under the standard inner product

open_problems: OGR-1: Does defect-guided refinement converge to behavioral equivalence for all finite systems?
OGR-2: Does the Jordan block structure undergo a phase transition as digit length increases?
OGR-3: Does D_Π^prob = 0 characterize exact lumpability for Markov chains?
